Center: OECD/NEA Nuclear Data Bank, Boulogne-Billancourt, France
ref: Jour: Jour. of Nuclear Science and Technology, Vol.61, p.161 (2024)
title: Measurements of keV-neutron capture cross Sections and capture gamma-ray spectra of 74,76,78,80,82Se

x4freetext: Measurements of keV-neutron capture cross Sections

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Science Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan

x4freetext: National Institute of Maritime, Port, and

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Jour. of Nuclear Science and Technology, Vol.61, p.161 (2024)

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Science Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan

x4freetext: The 7Li(p,n)7Be reaction with a pulsed proton
beam (1.5 ns neutron bunch width, 4 MHz repetition
The gamma rays were measured with a large
anti-Compton NaI(Tl) spectrometer using TOF. The main
detector of spectrometer (15.2 cm dia.,30.5 cm length),
was within a hollow Compton-suppression detector
The spectrometer was shielded with borated paraffin,
For the 15-100 keV range, a small 6Li-glass
detector (5.0 mm diameter, 5.0 mm thick) was placed
30 cm away from the 7Li neutron-generation target.
For the 550 keV measurements, a larger 6Li-glass
detector (102 mm diameter, 6.4 mm thick) was positioned
455 cm away from the neutron source.
Incident neutron energy spectrum was measured
A pulse-height weighting technique was used on
the observed gamma-ray spectra to determine capture

x4code.actHelp: Data received at the data center

x4freetext: Isotopically enriched Se metal powder used as sample:
The total uncertainty was the square root of
the sum of individual squared uncertainties.
Corrections were made for self-shielding and multiple
